Comment: Sets up S3 cross-account live replication on a source bucket owned by an external stack. Validates (never mutates) source versioning, then performs a SINGLE atomic read-merge-write on the one ReplicationConfiguration per bucket (WR-01): GetBucketReplication once, merge ALL Destinations[] Rules in one JSONata pass, PutBucketReplication once. PutBucketReplication replaces the whole config, so each spoke's Rule is keyed by a deterministic ID 'repl-<DestAccountId>-<DestBucketBasename>'; existing Rules for other spokes are preserved. Priority is stable per destination (CR-03): an existing rule's priority is reused, new destinations get max(existing priorities)+1, avoiding collisions on re-run. Every S3 Task assumes the source role imperatively via Credentials.RoleArn.$.

GetSourceVersioning.Comment: Validate-only versioning check (D-03). Reads the source bucket versioning status; never calls PutBucketVersioning.

IsVersioningEnabled.Comment: Gate on source bucket versioning. S3 replication requires versioning Enabled on the source.

VersioningNotEnabled.Cause: Source bucket versioning must be Enabled before configuring replication. The client stack that owns the source bucket must enable versioning; this module is validate-only (D-03) and never mutates the source bucket.

HasDestinations.Comment: Guard against an empty Destinations[] invocation (IN-04): short-circuit to a clear no-op rather than silently writing an unchanged config.

NoDestinationsProvided.Comment: No destinations supplied: nothing to configure. Reported explicitly so an empty-destinations invocation is not a silent success.

Parameters.Message: No destinations provided; no replication rules were added or changed.

ReadExistingConfig.Comment: Read the current ReplicationConfiguration ONCE before merging (WR-01: single atomic read-merge-write, no per-destination read-back). GetBucketReplication throws S3.ReplicationConfigurationNotFoundError ONLY when no config exists (Pitfall 1) -> seed an empty config. Any other error (throttle/AccessDenied/transient) MUST NOT seed an empty config, otherwise PutBucketReplication would overwrite every other spoke's Rule (CR-01); route those to SetupFailed.

SeedEmptyConfig.Comment: No existing replication config on the source bucket: seed an empty Rules array keyed by the replication role.

MergeAllRules.Comment: Single-pass merge (WR-01) of every destination into the existing Rules array. For each destination: compute its deterministic ID 'repl-<DestAccountId>-<DestBucketBasename>' (truncated to 255 chars). Reduce over Destinations[] starting from the existing Rules: for each destination, drop any existing Rule with the same ID, then append a freshly-built Rule. Priority is STABLE per destination (CR-03): if a Rule with this ID already exists, reuse its Priority; otherwise assign max(current accumulator priorities)+1. Because the reduce threads the accumulator, priorities assigned to new destinations within the same run are also collision-free. RTC forces Metrics (Pitfall 4); Filter requires ID+Status+Priority+DeleteMarkerReplication (Pitfall 3).

AssertDistinctPriorities.Comment: Defense-in-depth (CR-03): assert priorities are distinct across all merged Rules before PutBucketReplication. S3 V2 rules with a Filter require unique Priority; a duplicate would be rejected by the API. Fail fast with a clear error rather than surfacing an opaque PutBucketReplication rejection.

DuplicatePriority.Cause: The merged ReplicationConfiguration contains duplicate Rule priorities, which PutBucketReplication would reject. This indicates a priority-derivation bug (CR-03).

WriteConfig.Comment: Write the full merged Rules array back in a SINGLE PutBucketReplication (WR-01). PutBucketReplication replaces the entire ReplicationConfiguration (Pitfall 2), so the complete merged set is always written.

Parameters.Message: Cross-account replication configuration merged for all destinations on the source bucket in a single atomic write.

SetupFailed.Cause: Failed to set up S3 cross-account replication configuration.
